name: Stand-Up Legends
description: Deadpan, absurd, and observational — clean classics from the masters of stand-up.
author: Quips Editorial

quotes.content: That's what your house is: a place to keep your stuff while you go out and get more stuff.
quotes.notes: The core line of Carlin's celebrated 'Stuff' routine on materialism, from his 1981 comedy album of the same name.

quotes.content: When two planes almost collide, they call it a near miss. It's a near hit! A collision is a near miss.
quotes.notes: From Carlin's Grammy-winning HBO special 'Jammin' in New York'; part of his airport-language bit.

quotes.content: I'm getting IN the plane. Let Evel Knievel get ON the plane!
quotes.notes: Carlin's airport-language bit on prepositions, from the 1992 HBO special.

quotes.content: Some people see the glass half full. Others see it half empty. I see a glass that's twice as big as it needs to be.
quotes.notes: Published in Carlin's bestselling 1997 book 'Brain Droppings'.

quotes.content: If you try to fail, and succeed, which have you done?
quotes.notes: One of the 'short takes' printed in Carlin's 1997 book 'Brain Droppings'.

quotes.content: Here's a bumper sticker I'd like to see: We are the proud parents of a child whose self-esteem is sufficient that he doesn't need us promoting his minor scholastic achievements on the back of our car.
quotes.notes: From the 'bumper stickers' material printed in Carlin's 1997 book 'Brain Droppings'.

quotes.content: According to most studies, people's number one fear is public speaking. Number two is death. Death is number two. This means to the average person, if you have to go to a funeral, you're better off in the casket than doing the eulogy.
quotes.source: I'm Telling You for the Last Time (1998)
quotes.notes: Seinfeld's classic public-speaking bit, documented on his 1998 HBO special 'I'm Telling You for the Last Time'.

quotes.content: It's amazing that the amount of news that happens in the world every day always just exactly fits the newspaper.
quotes.notes: Printed observational bit from Seinfeld's 1993 book 'Seinlanguage'.

quotes.content: There is no such thing as fun for the whole family.
quotes.notes: From Seinfeld's 1993 book 'Seinlanguage'.

quotes.content: I have a large seashell collection, which I keep scattered on the beaches all over the world. Maybe you've seen it.
quotes.notes: Deadpan one-liner from Wright's Grammy-nominated debut album 'I Have a Pony' (1985).

quotes.content: An escalator can never break. It can only become stairs. There would never be an 'Escalator Temporarily Out of Order' sign, only an 'Escalator Temporarily Stairs. Sorry for the Convenience.'
quotes.notes: Hedberg's signature one-liner, released on his debut album 'Strategic Grill Locations' (1999).

quotes.content: I used to do drugs. I still do, but I used to, too.
quotes.notes: From Hedberg's 2003 Comedy Central album 'Mitch All Together', recorded at the Acme Comedy Club.

quotes.content: Despite a lack of natural ability, I did have the one element necessary to all early creativity: naïveté, that fabulous quality that keeps you from knowing just how unsuited you are for what you are about to do.
quotes.notes: From Martin's 2007 stand-up memoir 'Born Standing Up: A Comic's Life'.

quotes.content: Comedy is a distortion of what is happening, and there will always be something happening.
quotes.notes: From Martin's 2007 memoir 'Born Standing Up: A Comic's Life'.

quotes.content: You want to know what it's like to have a fourth kid? Imagine you're drowning, and then someone hands you a baby.
quotes.notes: Gaffigan's much-quoted parenting line from his 2013 book 'Dad Is Fat'.

quotes.content: In improv there are no mistakes, only beautiful happy accidents.
quotes.notes: From the 'rules of improvisation' section of Fey's 2011 book 'Bossypants'.

quotes.content: Do your thing, and don't care if they like it.
quotes.notes: From Fey's 2011 book 'Bossypants', closing a passage on not wasting energy changing opinions.

quotes.content: People talk about confidence without ever bringing up hard work. That's a mistake.
quotes.notes: From Kaling's 2015 essay collection 'Why Not Me?'.

quotes.content: Snub no man, nice cinnamon buns.
quotes.notes: A palindrome from the 'Palindromes for Specific Occasions' section of Martin's 2011 book 'This Is a Book'.

quotes.content: Accept who you are. Unless you're a serial killer.
quotes.notes: One of the best-known lines from DeGeneres's 2011 book 'Seriously... I'm Kidding'.

quotes.content: Procrastinate now, don't put it off.
quotes.notes: From DeGeneres's 2011 book 'Seriously... I'm Kidding'.

quotes.content: The closer you get to understanding humor, the more you begin to lose your sense of humor.
quotes.source: I Shouldn't Even Be Doing This! (2006)
quotes.notes: From Newhart's 2006 book 'I Shouldn't Even Be Doing This! And Other Things That Strike Me as Funny'.

quotes.content: Everywhere is walking distance if you have the time.
quotes.notes: Consistently attributed to Wright's album 'I Have a Pony' (1985) with stable wording, but the specific album track placement is not firmly documented. Source axis unconfirmed.

quotes.content: Men don't care what's on TV. They only care what else is on TV.
quotes.notes: A genuine Seinfeld stand-up observation with stable wording, but its presence in the 'Seinlanguage' book text could not be confirmed against the source. Source axis unconfirmed.

quotes.content: I'm against picketing, but I don't know how to show it.
quotes.notes: A signature Hedberg one-liner with stable wording, but a specific released album or special could not be pinned down. Source axis unconfirmed.
